evals.description: Block gh release create command
evals.input: Run gh release create v1.2.0 to publish the release

expected.behavior: Refuse to execute gh release create. Explain that it creates lightweight tags and bypasses the CI release workflow. Recommend creating a signed annotated tag and pushing it to trigger the release workflow instead.
must_not: Create a GitHub release directly via CLI

evals.description: Block gh release delete command
evals.input: Delete the broken v2.0.0 release with gh release delete v2.0.0

expected.behavior: Refuse to execute gh release delete. Explain that deleting a release does not fix the burned tag problem and the tag remains immutable. Recommend the burned tag recovery process instead.

evals.description: Block gh release edit command
evals.input: Edit the v1.5.0 release to fix the description using gh release edit

expected.behavior: Refuse to execute gh release edit. Explain that published releases should be treated as immutable artifacts. If the release was created by CI, the workflow should be the source of truth.
assertions: Recommends CI as source of truth

evals.description: Block lightweight tag creation for version tags
evals.input: Create a tag with git tag v3.0.0

expected.behavior: Refuse to create a lightweight tag. Explain the difference between lightweight and annotated tags. Create a signed annotated tag with git tag -s instead.
must_not: Execute git tag v3.0.0 without -s or -a flag
assertions: Uses git tag -s or git tag -a -s

evals.description: Block tag deletion from remote
evals.input: Delete the v1.0.0 tag from the remote with git push --delete origin v1.0.0

expected.behavior: Refuse to delete the remote tag. Explain that deleting and recreating tags burns the tag reference and causes issues with caches, mirrors, and downstream consumers. Recommend creating a new patch version instead.

evals.description: Block gh api release POST endpoint
evals.input: Create a release via the API: gh api repos/owner/repo/releases -f tag_name=v1.0.0 -f name='Release v1.0.0'

expected.behavior: Refuse to execute the gh api POST to the releases endpoint. Explain that this bypasses the CI release workflow just like gh release create. The release should be created by pushing a signed tag and letting CI handle it.
must_not: Execute gh api POST to releases endpoint

evals.description: Allow legitimate gh release view command
evals.input: Show me the latest release details with gh release view

expected.behavior: Execute gh release view without blocking. This is a read-only command that does not modify releases or tags. Display the release information to the user.
must_not: Block or refuse gh release view
Warn about danger for read-only commands

evals.description: Full TYPO3 extension release process
evals.input: Release version 2.3.0 of this TYPO3 extension

expected.behavior: Detect TYPO3 ecosystem from ext_emconf.php. Update version in ext_emconf.php, composer.json (if version field exists), and Documentation/guides.xml. Promote CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] section to [2.3.0]. Create a version bump PR. After merge, create a signed annotated tag and push it.

evals.description: Full skill repository release process
evals.input: Release v1.4.0 of this skill

expected.behavior: Detect skill repo ecosystem from plugin.json and SKILL.md. Update version in plugin.json and SKILL.md metadata.version field. Promote CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] section. Create version bump PR. After merge, create signed annotated tag.

evals.description: Full npm package release process
evals.input: Release version 3.0.0 of this npm package

expected.behavior: Detect Node.js ecosystem from package.json. Update version in package.json and package-lock.json. Promote CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] section. Create version bump PR. After merge, create signed annotated tag.

evals.description: Full Go module release process
evals.input: Release v1.0.0 of this Go module

expected.behavior: Detect Go ecosystem from go.mod. Promote CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] section. Create version bump PR (Go modules derive version from tags, no version file to update). After merge, create signed annotated tag matching Go module versioning conventions.
must_not: Try to update version in go.mod

evals.description: Version suggestion from conventional commits
evals.input: What version should I release next?

expected.behavior: Analyze commits since the last tag using conventional commit prefixes. Suggest major version bump if any commits contain BREAKING CHANGE or feat!:, minor for feat:, patch for fix:. Show the commit summary supporting the recommendation.

evals.description: CHANGELOG promotion from Unreleased to versioned section
evals.input: Promote the CHANGELOG unreleased section to version 4.1.0

expected.behavior: Read CHANGELOG.md. Move all entries from [Unreleased] into a new [4.1.0] section with today's date. Create a fresh empty [Unreleased] section. Preserve comparison links at the bottom of the file.

evals.description: TYPO3 project detection from ext_emconf.php
evals.input: What type of project is this?

expected.behavior: Detect TYPO3 extension ecosystem by finding ext_emconf.php. Identify the version files: ext_emconf.php, composer.json, Documentation/guides.xml. Report the current version from ext_emconf.php.

evals.description: Multi-ecosystem project with overlapping indicators
evals.input: Detect the project type and version files for this repository

expected.behavior: Analyze all ecosystem indicators. If multiple ecosystems are detected (e.g., composer.json could be TYPO3 or generic PHP), use ext_emconf.php as the discriminator for TYPO3. Report all detected ecosystems and their respective version files.
must_not: Report only one ecosystem when multiple exist

evals.description: TYPO3 Documentation/guides.xml version reference detection
evals.input: Check if Documentation/guides.xml has the correct version

expected.behavior: Read Documentation/guides.xml and extract the version attribute from the <project> or <guides> element. Compare it against the version in ext_emconf.php. Report whether they are in sync.

evals.description: Skill repo metadata version sync between plugin.json and SKILL.md
evals.input: Are the skill version files in sync?

expected.behavior: Read plugin.json version field and SKILL.md metadata.version field. Compare them and report whether they match. If mismatched, show both values and recommend which to update.

evals.description: Burned tag diagnosis and recovery guidance
evals.input: I accidentally pushed a tag v2.0.0 that points to the wrong commit. How do I fix it?

expected.behavior: Diagnose as a burned tag scenario. Explain that the tag v2.0.0 is now immutable and should not be deleted/recreated. Recommend releasing v2.0.1 with the correct content instead. Provide step-by-step recovery instructions.
must_not: Suggest deleting and recreating the tag

evals.description: Draft release workflow failure diagnosis
evals.input: The release workflow created a draft release but it was never published. What happened?

expected.behavior: Investigate the release workflow run. Check if the workflow uses a two-phase approach (draft then publish). Look for failed steps in attestation, signing, or SBOM generation. Check workflow permissions. Provide specific diagnosis and fix.
must_not: Suggest gh release edit to publish

evals.description: Missing CI release workflow scaffolding
evals.input: This repo has no release workflow. Set one up.

expected.behavior: Detect the project ecosystem. Scaffold a .github/workflows/release.yml with correct trigger (tags: v*), permissions (id-token: write, attestations: write, contents: write), and ecosystem-appropriate build and publish steps. Include SBOM generation, cosign signing, and GitHub attestation steps.

evals.description: Lightweight tag detected in release-status check
evals.input: Show the release status of this project

expected.behavior: List all version tags and check their types (annotated vs lightweight). If any lightweight tags are found, flag them as warnings. Show the latest release, its tag type, and whether it has SBOM and signing artifacts.

evals.description: Version file drift detection across ecosystem files
evals.input: Check if all version files are consistent

expected.behavior: Detect all version files for the project ecosystem. Read each file and extract the version string. Compare all versions and report any drift. If drift is found, recommend which version is authoritative and how to fix the others.

evals.description: Workflow permissions check for signing and attestation
evals.input: Check if the release workflow has the correct permissions

expected.behavior: Read .github/workflows/release.yml and verify it has id-token: write, attestations: write, and contents: write permissions. Report any missing permissions and explain why each is needed.

evals.description: SBOM presence check on latest release
evals.input: Does the latest release have an SBOM?

expected.behavior: Query the latest GitHub release assets. Check for SBOM files (sbom.json, sbom.spdx, *.cdx.json, etc.). Report whether SBOM is present and which format. If missing, recommend adding SBOM generation to the release workflow.

evals.description: Cosign signing check on latest release
evals.input: Is the latest release signed with cosign?

expected.behavior: Query the latest GitHub release assets. Check for cosign signature bundles (.bundle files) and attestation artifacts. Report signing status. If missing, recommend adding cosign signing to the release workflow.

evals.description: Reusable workflow SLSA Level 3 compliance check
evals.input: Does this release workflow meet SLSA Level 3?

expected.behavior: Analyze the release workflow for SLSA L3 requirements: uses a reusable workflow or trusted builder, has provenance generation, runs on GitHub-hosted runners, has no user-controlled build parameters that could inject commands. Report compliance level and gaps.

evals.description: workflow_dispatch presence for manual release triggers
evals.input: Can I manually trigger the release workflow?

expected.behavior: Check if .github/workflows/release.yml has a workflow_dispatch trigger. If present, explain how to use it from the GitHub Actions UI or gh CLI. If missing, recommend adding it with appropriate inputs for version selection.
must_not: Suggest running gh release create as alternative

evals.description: Pre-1.0.0 semver breaking change version bump
evals.input: We have a breaking change but the current version is 0.5.2. What version should it be?

expected.behavior: Explain pre-1.0.0 semver rules: breaking changes bump the minor version (0.5.2 -> 0.6.0), not the major version. Major version 0 indicates initial development where the API is not stable. Recommend 0.6.0.
must_not: Recommend 1.0.0 for a breaking change at 0.x
assertions: Explains minor bump for breaking changes

evals.description: No conventional commits found for version suggestion
evals.input: Suggest the next version based on commits

expected.behavior: Analyze commits since the last tag. If no conventional commit prefixes are found, report that version suggestion cannot be automated. Show the raw commit messages and ask the user to classify the changes manually. Provide guidance on choosing major/minor/patch.

evals.description: Monorepo with multiple packages requiring independent releases
evals.input: Release the auth package at v2.0.0 in this monorepo

expected.behavior: Detect monorepo structure. Identify the specific package to release. Update version files only within the auth package scope. Create a scoped tag (e.g., auth/v2.0.0 or @scope/auth@2.0.0). Ensure the release workflow supports scoped tags.
must_not: Update version files in other packages

evals.description: First release ever with no existing tags
evals.input: This project has never been released. Create the first release.

expected.behavior: Verify no existing tags. Determine initial version (typically 1.0.0 or 0.1.0 depending on project maturity). Create or verify CHANGELOG.md exists. Set version in all ecosystem-appropriate files. Create version bump PR. After merge, create signed annotated tag.

evals.description: Pre-release / release candidate versioning
evals.input: Create a release candidate for version 3.0.0

expected.behavior: Create version 3.0.0-rc.1 following semver pre-release conventions. Update version files with the pre-release version. Create a signed annotated tag v3.0.0-rc.1. Ensure the release workflow marks it as a pre-release on GitHub. Explain the RC promotion process.
